name: Pyramidal Horn
description: A pyramidal horn antenna is a flared waveguide structure that provides a smooth transition from a rectangular waveguide to free space. It features flaring in both E-plane and H-plane dimensions, creating a pyramidal shape that improves impedance matching and directivity compared to an open waveguide. The horn provides moderate to high gain with good impedance matching over a wide frequency range.

a.name: Waveguide Width
a.description: Width of the rectangular waveguide feeding the horn

b.name: Waveguide Height
b.description: Height of the rectangular waveguide feeding the horn

A.name: Horn Aperture Width
A.description: Width of the horn aperture in the E-plane

B.name: Horn Aperture Height
B.description: Height of the horn aperture in the H-plane

L1.name: E-plane Horn Length
L1.description: Axial length of the horn in the E-plane

L2.name: H-plane Horn Length
L2.description: Axial length of the horn in the H-plane

flare_angle_E.name: E-plane Flare Angle
flare_angle_E.description: Half-angle of flare in the E-plane

flare_angle_H.name: H-plane Flare Angle
flare_angle_H.description: Half-angle of flare in the H-plane

designMethodology.overview: Design of pyramidal horn antennas involves determining optimal aperture dimensions and horn lengths to achieve desired gain and beamwidth while maintaining good impedance matching. The design balances aperture efficiency against phase error from the horn geometry.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency and Waveguide Dimensions
steps.description: Select the operating frequency and corresponding rectangular waveguide dimensions for the dominant TE10 mode
steps.formula: a = 0.7 * lambda, b = 0.35 * lambda, fc = c / (2 * a)

steps.title: Calculate Desired Gain and Aperture Area
steps.description: Determine the required aperture area based on desired gain using aperture antenna theory
steps.formula: G = 4 * pi * Aeff / lambda^2, Aeff = eta_ap * A * B

steps.title: Optimize E-plane Dimensions
steps.description: Calculate optimal E-plane aperture width and horn length for maximum gain with acceptable phase error
steps.formula: A_opt = sqrt(3 * lambda * L1), L1 = (A^2 - a^2) / (8 * lambda)

steps.title: Optimize H-plane Dimensions
steps.description: Calculate optimal H-plane aperture height and horn length for maximum gain with acceptable phase error
steps.formula: B_opt = sqrt(2 * lambda * L2), L2 = (B^2 - b^2) / (8 * lambda)

steps.title: Calculate Flare Angles
steps.description: Determine the flare angles in both planes to ensure gradual transition and good impedance matching
steps.formula: theta_E = atan((A - a) / (2 * L1)), theta_H = atan((B - b) / (2 * L2))

steps.title: Verify Phase Error and Aperture Efficiency
steps.description: Check that phase errors are acceptable and calculate aperture efficiency
steps.formula: delta_E = (A - a)^2 / (8 * lambda * L1), delta_H = (B - b)^2 / (8 * lambda * L2)

steps.title: Calculate Radiation Patterns and Gain
steps.description: Compute the theoretical radiation patterns and gain using aperture field distribution
steps.formula: G = 32 * A * B / (lambda^2 * theta_E * theta_H), theta_3dB = 51 * lambda / A (E-plane)

equations.resonantFrequency: fc = c / (2 * a) for TE10 mode cutoff
equations.gain: G = 32 * A * B / (lambda^2 * theta_E_rad * theta_H_rad) where theta in radians

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line model with gradual taper approximation, account for TE10 mode propagation and reflection coefficient at aperture
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply Huygens principle with uniform amplitude and quadratic phase distribution across aperture, use Fresnel integrals for pattern calculation

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 13
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 11
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 15
Love, A.W., 'Electromagnetic Horn Antennas', IEEE Press, 1976
